danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures throughout obstacle courses
danger_signals.meaning: Areas contain direct threats requiring combat or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards embedded in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that damage or kill the player upon contact
Player survival status; depletes without collecting tropical fruit

danger_signals.visual_form: Evil sorcerer character in narrative/intro sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; source of world peril (Tina's petrification)

danger_signals.visual_form: Distant goal across the sea; final stage setting
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous destination where maximum threat concentrates

safety_signals.meaning: Resource for maintaining health; visible recovery mechanism
Offensive capability increase; progression toward combat readiness
Mobility enhancement; faster traversal reduces exposure time

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual objective at end of obstacle course sections

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Adventure Island (home territory); treetop starting location

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil sorcerer visual identity; petrification curse signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss battle form at Ice Mountain final stage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Female companion character; petrified statue form represents mission objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Higgins and Tina stargazing/resting atop treetop in intro sequence
Tina transformed into stone statue; Dark Cloak retreats to Ice Mountain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higgins resolves to quest; player enters combat/platforming mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Obstacle course environments; enemy placements; trap configurations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Navigation through hostile terrain; combat with enemies; collection of resources

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dedicated boss battle arena; unique enemy visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Concentrated threat; pattern-based combat; greater difficulty spike

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tropical fruit visibility in level design; weapon pickups scattered throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can restore health; increase offensive capability; prepare for danger ahead

environmental_storytelling.example: Cinematic showing Higgins and Tina's peaceful moment interrupted by Dark Cloak's curse, establishing plot motivation without dialogue
Adventure Island as safe home vs. Ice Mountain as hostile unknown territory; physical distance represents danger escalation
Tina's transformation into stone statue visually communicates permanence of threat and urgency of quest
Five stages with four areas each; three obstacle courses followed by boss battle; structure mirrors hero's journey toward confrontation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Environmental hazards as world danger expression
environmental_storytelling.example: Tropical setting filled with traps and enemies; visualizes hostile nature of world requiring weapons/resources

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard platforming traversal; slow base movement speed; can crouch and super-jump
Can fire projectiles (axe or boomerang) in limited directions; offensive capability unlocked
Can fire multiple consecutive shots (up to 3); increased offensive throughput
Projectiles transform into fireballs; capable of destroying stone obstacles; persistent effect across weapon swaps until death
Increased traversal speed; reduced time exposure to hazards
Visual indicator of vulnerability; requires fruit collection to reverse; death occurs at zero health
Animated movement patterns throughout stages; pose continuous threat
Boss battle behavior; unique attack patterns; concentrated threat at Ice Mountain

notes: Super Adventure Island communicates world-state primarily through character transformation (Tina's petrification establishing crisis), environmental design (Adventure Island as safe home vs. Ice Mountain as hostile endpoint), and gameplay mechanics as danger expression (fruit preservation as survival mechanic, weapon progression as increasing capability). The game uses minimal faction complexity—essentially a binary hero/villain narrative with ally objective. Visual storytelling relies on sprite-based character presentation and level design rather than explicit UI signals. Regional differences (infinite vs. 2 continues in Japanese version) affect difficulty communication but not visual manifestation. The narrative frame (intro cinema) establishes all political/emotional context before gameplay begins.
